<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="copilot_applyPatch">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **copilot_applyPatch** • Generating patch (64 lines) in [](file:///Users/martian_elk/Projects/clinical_lab_analyzer/csv_uploader/csv_uploader.py)</summary>

```python
"""
CLI uploader for canonical analyzer CSV to ingestion API
The uploader detects new exports and pushes them to
POST /v1/ingestions on a schedule
"""

import argparse
import requests
import time
import hashlib
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime
import yaml

from requests.exceptions import ConnectionError as RequestsConnectionError
from requests.exceptions import Timeout as RequestsTimeout
from requests.exceptions import RequestException

WATCH_DIR = Path("csv_uploader/simulated_exports/pending")
PROCESSED_DIR = Path("csv_uploader/simulated_exports/uploaded")
FAILED_DIR = Path("csv_uploader/simulated_exports/failed")
CONFIG_FILE_PATH = Path("csv_uploader/config.yaml")

STABILITY_DELAY_SECONDS = 10  # Time to wait for file to be stable
POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 20
CSV_POST_API_ENDPOINT = "/v1/ingestions"
UPLOADER_ID = "uploader_001"
REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30
MAX_UPLOAD_RETRIES = 3
RETRY_BACKOFF_SECONDS = 3


def _parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description=(
            "Watch a folder for analyzer CSVs and upload them to the ingestion API. "
            "Supports one-shot and single-file modes for benchmarks."
        )
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--file",
        action="append",
        default=None,
        help=(
            "Upload a specific CSV file path. Can be provided multiple times. "
            "If set, the uploader will process only these file(s) once and exit."
        ),
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--once",
        action="store_true",
        help="Process current CSV files once, then exit.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--watch-dir",
        default=str(WATCH_DIR),
        help="Directory to scan for pending CSV files.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--processed-dir",
        default=str(PROCESSED_DIR),
        help="Directory to move successfully uploaded CSV files.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--failed-dir",
        default=str(FAILED_DIR),
        help="Directory to move failed uploads.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--poll-interval-seconds",
        type=int,
        default=POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS,
        help="Seconds to wait between polls when watching.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--stability-delay-seconds",
        type=int,
        default=STABILITY_DELAY_SECONDS,
        help="Seconds a file must be unchanged before uploading (0 disables).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--timeout-seconds",
        type=int,
        default=REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
        help="HTTP request timeout in seconds.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--max-upload-retries",
        type=int,
        default=MAX_UPLOAD_RETRIES,
        help="Max retry attempts for connection/timeout errors.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--retry-backoff-seconds",
        type=int,
        default=RETRY_BACKOFF_SECONDS,
        help="Base backoff seconds; attempt N sleeps backoff*N.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--debug-request",
        action="store_true",
        help="Print request details (URL + form fields).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--keep-files",
        action="store_true",
        help=(
            "Do not move files after upload attempt (useful for benchmark fixtures). "
            "Combine with --once to avoid re-uploading in a loop."
        ),
    )
    return parser.parse_args()


def read_config() -> dict:
    """Reads configuration data from a YAML file."""
    with open(CONFIG_FILE_PATH, "r") as file:
        config_data = yaml.safe_load(file)
        return config_data


def process_file(
    *,
    csv_path: Path,
    config: dict,
    session: requests.Session,
    processed_dir: Path,
    failed_dir: Path,
    stability_delay_seconds: int,
    request_timeout_seconds: int,
    max_upload_retries: int,
    retry_backoff_seconds: int,
    debug_request: bool,
    keep_files: bool,
) -> None:
    """Processes a single CSV file: computes hash, uploads, and optionally moves it."""
    print(f"Processing {csv_path.name}...")

    # Ensure file is stable
    if stability_delay_seconds > 0:
        while True:
            last_modified_time = csv_path.stat().st_mtime
            time_since_last_modification = time.time() - last_modified_time
            if time_since_last_modification > stability_delay_seconds:
                print(
                    f"File {csv_path.name} is stable (age: {time_since_last_modification:.2f}s)."
                )
                break
            print(
                f"File {csv_path.name} is too new (age: {time_since_last_modification:.2f}s). Waiting..."
            )
            time.sleep(stability_delay_seconds)

    # Get current time as a string
    uploader_received_at = datetime.now().isoformat()
    # Get run_id from CSV file name
    run_id = csv_path.stem
    print(f"Extracted run id: {run_id}")
    # Compute SHA-256
    sha256_hash = hashlib.sha256()

    try:
        with open(csv_path, "rb") as f:
            # Read and update hash in chunks to handle large files
            for byte_block in iter(lambda: f.read(4096), b""):
                sha256_hash.update(byte_block)
            content_sha256 = sha256_hash.hexdigest()
            print(f"content_sha256: {content_sha256}")

            # Rewind file to be sent via request
            f.seek(0)

            # Upload the file
            url = config["api_base_url"].rstrip("/") + CSV_POST_API_ENDPOINT
            files = {"file": (csv_path.name, f, "text/csv")}
            data = {
                "uploader_id": UPLOADER_ID,
                "spec_version": config["spec_version"],
                "instrument_id": config["instrument_id"],
                "run_id": run_id,
                "content_sha256": content_sha256,
                "uploader_received_at": uploader_received_at,
            }

            if debug_request:
                print("--- Preparing HTTP Request ---")
                print(f"URL: POST {url}")
                print(f"Data: {data}")
                print(f"Files: {files['file'][0]}")
                print("-----------------------------")

            print(f"Uploading to {url}...")

            response = None
            for attempt in range(1, max_upload_retries + 1):
                try:
                    # Ensure file content is replayable for retries
                    f.seek(0)
                    response = session.post(
                        url,
                        files=files,
                        data=data,
                        timeout=request_timeout_seconds,
                    )
                    break
                except (RequestsConnectionError, RequestsTimeout) as e:
                    print(
                        f"Upload attempt {attempt}/{max_upload_retries} failed: {e}"
                    )
                    if attempt < max_upload_retries:
                        time.sleep(retry_backoff_seconds * attempt)
                    else:
                        # Keep the file in pending so it can be retried on the next poll.
                        print(
                            f"API not reachable; leaving {csv_path.name} in pending for retry."
                        )
                        return
                except RequestException as e:
                    # Non-connection related requests failures are treated as failures.
                    print(f"Request failed: {e}")
                    if keep_files:
                        print("Keeping file in place (--keep-files).")
                    else:
                        failed_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                        csv_path.rename(failed_dir / csv_path.name)
                        print(f"Moved to {failed_dir}")
                    return

        assert response is not None

        print(f"API Response: {response.status_code}")
        # response.ok means that response code is < 400
        if response.ok:
            print(f"Response: {response.json()}")
            # Move file to processed directory on successful upload
            if keep_files:
                print("Keeping file in place (--keep-files).")
            else:
                processed_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                csv_path.rename(processed_dir / csv_path.name)
                print(f"Moved to {processed_dir}")
        else:
            print(f"Error: {response.text}")
            if keep_files:
                print("Keeping file in place (--keep-files).")
            else:
                failed_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                csv_path.rename(failed_dir / csv_path.name)
                print(f"Moved to {failed_dir}")

    except IOError as e:
        print(f"Error processing file {csv_path}: {e}")
        if keep_files:
            print("Keeping file in place (--keep-files).")
        else:
            failed_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            csv_path.rename(failed_dir / csv_path.name)
            print(f"Moved to {failed_dir}")


def main() -> None:
    try:
        args = _parse_args()
        config = read_config()

        watch_dir = Path(args.watch_dir)
        processed_dir = Path(args.processed_dir)
        failed_dir = Path(args.failed_dir)

        # Reuse connections across many uploads (important for batch benchmarks)
        session = requests.Session()

        if args.file:
            csv_files = [Path(p) for p in args.file]
            missing = [str(p) for p in csv_files if not p.exists()]
            if missing:
                raise FileNotFoundError(
                    "CSV file(s) not found: " + ", ".join(missing)
                )

            # Process explicit files once, then exit.
            for csv_path in sorted(csv_files, key=lambda p: p.name):
                process_file(
                    csv_path=csv_path,
                    config=config,
                    session=session,
                    processed_dir=processed_dir,
                    failed_dir=failed_dir,
                    stability_delay_seconds=args.stability_delay_seconds,
                    request_timeout_seconds=args.timeout_seconds,
                    max_upload_retries=args.max_upload_retries,
                    retry_backoff_seconds=args.retry_backoff_seconds,
                    debug_request=args.debug_request,
                    keep_files=args.keep_files,
                )
            return

        print(f"Watching for new CSV files in {watch_dir}...")

        while True:
            csv_files = sorted(watch_dir.glob("*.csv"))
            if not csv_files:
                if args.once:
                    return

                time.sleep(args.poll_interval_seconds)
                continue

            for csv_path in csv_files:
                process_file(
                    csv_path=csv_path,
                    config=config,
                    session=session,
                    processed_dir=processed_dir,
                    failed_dir=failed_dir,
                    stability_delay_seconds=args.stability_delay_seconds,
                    request_timeout_seconds=args.timeout_seconds,
                    max_upload_retries=args.max_upload_retries,
                    retry_backoff_seconds=args.retry_backoff_seconds,
                    debug_request=args.debug_request,
                    keep_files=args.keep_files,
                )

            if args.once:
                return

            time.sleep(args.poll_interval_seconds)
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        print("\nWatcher stopped.")
    except FileNotFoundError:
        print(f"Error: Configuration file not found at {CONFIG_FILE_PATH}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"An unexpected error occurred: {e}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

```


</details>
</tool-use>

**Considering file processing options**

The user is asking if they can point to a specific file. It seems they want a simple command-line interface (CLI) option. I’m thinking of adding a `--file` argument which can accept multiple files and is mutually exclusive with watch-dir. If `--file` is given, I’ll process those files once and exit, while keeping the existing behavior intact. I'll also consider what to do if files are outside the designated folders, ensuring we comply with expected behaviors.**Updating file handling options**

I'm considering how to handle multiple files with the new `--file` argument, like `--file path1 --file path2`. I’ll want to build a list of files in the main function if any are provided. I also need to validate their existence and call the processing function for each file. In this mode, I shouldn't loop or poll—just exit after processing. I’ll update the help string to indicate this supports specific file uploads and make necessary adjustments, including minimal tests.Yep — I’ll add a `--file` option to the uploader so you can upload one specific CSV (or multiple `--file` args) without relying on directory scanning, and it’ll still respect `--keep-files` so nothing gets moved if you don’t want it to.Now I’ll quickly re-run `--help` so you can see the new `--file` flag and confirm it’s usable as intended.Yes — I added support for uploading a specific file.

Use:

- Upload one file, don’t move it:
  - `uv run python -m csv_uploader.csv_uploader --file path/to/one.csv --keep-files`

- Upload multiple specific files:
  - `uv run python -m csv_uploader.csv_uploader --file a.csv --file b.csv --file c.csv --keep-files`

Behavior:
- If `--file` is provided, the uploader processes **only those file(s) once and exits** (it bypasses `--watch-dir` scanning).
- `--keep-files` ensures the uploader will not move/rename the file(s) after success/failure.

Change is in `csv_uploader.py`.

I’ll check your Compose/Postgres configuration (db name/user/port/volume) so I can tell you the exact `psql`/connection string to access the data, and explain why `down -v` makes the DB look “new” after resets.**Exploring Docker database access**

I need to access existing DB data in Docker, so I'll skip using the `-v` option because it removes the volume on reset. Instead, I can check the volume name, like `clinical_lab_analyzer_db-data`, with `docker volume ls`. To connect, I could use the command `docker compose exec db psql -U postgres -d cla`. Since port 5432 is mapped, I can also access it from the host as `psql postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/cla`. If I want to maintain data across resets, I'll use `docker compose down` instead of `down -v`.**Explaining Docker and Postgres**

It seems they've been using CLA on Postgres but didn't realize that Docker creates a new database each time it's reset. I need to clarify that containers are ephemeral while volumes can be persistent. It’s essential to not use the `-v` flag if they want to keep their data. I’ll recommend using an external GUI like DBeaver for convenience and provide safe commands formatted with backticks. Also, if they reset with `down -v`, previous data will be lost unless backups exist. Checking the Docker volume list can confirm if the volume is still there.Docker isn’t creating a “new DB” arbitrarily — your *reset command* is deleting the Postgres **volume**.

From `compose.yaml`, Postgres stores its data in the named volume `db-data`:

- `volumes: - db-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data`

So:

- `docker compose down -v` **deletes `db-data`** → next `up` starts Postgres with an empty data directory → it re-creates the `cla` database fresh.
- `docker compose down` (no `-v`) stops/removes containers but **keeps `db-data`** → data is still there when you `up` again.

## How to access the DB data (while the stack is running)

Because your Compose maps `5432:5432`, you can connect either via Docker exec or from your host.

### Option A (most reliable): exec into the db container
- `docker compose exec db psql -U postgres -d cla`

Then inside psql:
- `\\dt` (list tables)
- `select * from ingestion order by api_received_at desc limit 5;`

### Option B: connect from macOS host (psql / GUI tools)
Connection string:
- `psql "postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/cla"`

GUI (DBeaver / pgAdmin):
- host: `localhost`
- port: `5432`
- db: `cla`
- user: `postgres`
- password: `postgres`

## If you want to keep the data after you’re done
- Use `docker compose down` (no `-v`).

## If you need both: reset for benchmarks but still inspect results later
Before you wipe with `down -v`, dump it:
- `docker compose exec -T db pg_dump -U postgres -d cla > cla_dump.sql`

If you tell me whether you want “inspect last run’s DB” vs “keep a history of runs”, I can suggest a clean workflow (e.g., dump per run, or separate volumes per phase).

_**User (2026-03-20 19:38Z)**_

I set the folder with fixes csvs to read-only, is it recommended to also create hashes of the files? or is it overkill for a resume project?

---

_**Agent (model copilot/gpt-5.2)**_

Creating hashes is a good “lightweight rigor” move and not overkill here — but keep it proportional.

For a resume project, the best tradeoff is:

- Yes: record a SHA-256 for each fixed CSV **once**, and commit that manifest.
- No: you don’t need heavy tooling, signing, or complicated provenance pipelines.

Why it’s worth it (even for a resume project)
- It proves your “before vs after” runs used the exact same inputs.
- It makes results reproducible for a reviewer (or future you).
- It’s cheap: one small `hashes.json`/`hashes.txt` file.

What I’d recommend you do
- Create a simple manifest (committed) listing:
  - filename
  - bytes / row count (optional but nice)
  - SHA-256
- Keep the CSVs read-only as you already did.

One-liner options (pick one)
- macOS built-in:
  - `shasum -a 256 metrics/data_raw/fixed_csv_v1/*.csv > metrics/data_raw/fixed_csv_v1/SHA256SUMS.txt`
- Cross-platform-ish (Python):
  - `uv run python -c "import glob,hashlib; from pathlib import Path; import sys; files=sorted(glob.glob('metrics/data_raw/fixed_csv_v1/*.csv'));\nfor f in files:\n p=Path(f); h=hashlib.sha256(p.read_bytes()).hexdigest(); print(h, p.name)" > metrics/data_raw/fixed_csv_v1/SHA256SUMS.txt`

When it’s overkill
- If you start hashing every generated run artifact, or building a full data lineage system. For benchmarks, hashing just the fixed dataset is the sweet spot.

If you want, I can add a short “Dataset integrity” subsection to `README.md` with the exact command + what to commit.

_**Agent (model copilot/gpt-5.2)**_

**Normalizing SQL for Fingerprinting**

I'll provide an example focusing on top statements sorted by total time and count, along with a minimal code snippet that includes a QueryStats dataclass for counters. I’ll also need to make sure to attach listeners to the app's engine while adhering to the file linkification rules. However, I need to be cautious about not mixing formatting styles for file references.**Setting Up Symbols and Monitoring Metrics**

For symbols, I can use backticks, but I need to remember not to mix monospace and bold styles. Backticks work for commands too. I want to provide a Python code snippet that’s not in a file. My approach seems right — calculating total database time involves summing statement durations. For wall clock measurements, I can check client timing around POST requests. I must be cautious with global event listeners, ensuring they're installed once. For metrics, using `run_id` isn’t necessary. I'll confirm the approach and show how to use `ContextVar` in benchmarking. If using background tasks, logging DB stats separately per ingestion ID is best.**Providing Code Examples**

The user is asking for a code example, so I can present two options. First, I could show how to store server-side context in a context variable during the ingestion process, allowing for computation of database metrics within that task. For end-to-end measurement, I could provide a client script that measures wall clock time. Additionally, for server database stats, I’d suggest storing aggregated results in logs that are keyed by ingestion ID. I’ll keep everything brief since no extra tools are necessary.Yes — SQLAlchemy’s `before_cursor_execute` / `after_cursor_execute` event listeners are a solid approach for:

- Total SQL query count
- Total DB time (sum of per-statement durations)
- Query fingerprints + “top statements” by total time / count

One important nuance: those listeners fire for *all* DB activity on that SQLAlchemy `Engine`, so you typically pair them with a “current run collector” (per ingestion/run) so you only attribute queries that happen during the run you’re measuring.

Below is a concrete, minimal pattern that works well.

---

**Code Example (instrumentation + usage)**

```python
# metrics_query_listener.py
from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from contextvars import ContextVar
import re
import time
from typing import Any

from sqlalchemy import event
from sqlalchemy.engine import Engine

# Holds the active collector for the current logical run (if any).
_ACTIVE_COLLECTOR: ContextVar["QueryCollector | None"] = ContextVar(
    "ACTIVE_QUERY_COLLECTOR",
    default=None,
)

_LITERAL_RE = re.compile(
    r"""
    (?:'(?:''|[^'])*')         # single-quoted strings
    |(?:\b\d+(?:\.\d+)?\b)     # ints/floats
    """,
    re.VERBOSE,
)

_WS_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")


def fingerprint_sql(statement: str) -> str:
    """
    Coarse SQL fingerprinting:
    - collapse whitespace
    - replace literal strings/numbers with '?'
    This is enough for “top statements” views without heavy dependencies.
    """
    s = statement.strip()
    s = _WS_RE.sub(" ", s)
    s = _LITERAL_RE.sub("?", s)
    return s


@dataclass
class QueryCollector:
    query_count: int = 0
    total_db_time_s: float = 0.0
    # fingerprint -> total time
    fingerprint_time_s: dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=lambda: defaultdict(float))
    # fingerprint -> count
    fingerprint_count: Counter[str] = field(default_factory=Counter)

    def record(self, statement: str, duration_s: float) -> None:
        fp = fingerprint_sql(statement)
        self.query_count += 1
        self.total_db_time_s += duration_s
        self.fingerprint_time_s[fp] += duration_s
        self.fingerprint_count[fp] += 1

    def top_by_time(self, n: int = 10) -> list[tuple[str, float, int]]:
        items = [
            (fp, self.fingerprint_time_s[fp], self.fingerprint_count[fp])
            for fp in self.fingerprint_time_s
        ]
        return sorted(items, key=lambda t: t[1], reverse=True)[:n]

    def top_by_count(self, n: int = 10) -> list[tuple[str, float, int]]:
        items = [
            (fp, self.fingerprint_time_s[fp], self.fingerprint_count[fp])
            for fp in self.fingerprint_count
        ]
        return sorted(items, key=lambda t: t[2], reverse=True)[:n]


def install_query_listeners(engine: Engine) -> None:
    """
    Install global listeners exactly once per Engine.
    They will only record when _ACTIVE_COLLECTOR is set.
    """

    @event.listens_for(engine, "before_cursor_execute")
    def _before_cursor_execute(
        conn, cursor, statement: str, parameters: Any, context, executemany: bool
    ) -> None:
        # Store a high-res start time on the per-execution context.
        context._query_start_time_s = time.perf_counter()

    @event.listens_for(engine, "after_cursor_execute")
    def _after_cursor_execute(
        conn, cursor, statement: str, parameters: Any, context, executemany: bool
    ) -> None:
        collector = _ACTIVE_COLLECTOR.get()
        if collector is None:
            return

        start = getattr(context, "_query_start_time_s", None)
        if start is None:
            return

        duration_s = time.perf_counter() - start
        collector.record(statement, duration_s)


class collect_queries:
    """
    Context manager for one logical benchmark run.
    """

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self.collector = QueryCollector()
        self._token = None

    def __enter__(self) -> QueryCollector:
        self._token = _ACTIVE_COLLECTOR.set(self.collector)
        return self.collector

    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb) -> None:
        if self._token is not None:
            _ACTIVE_COLLECTOR.reset(self._token)
```

**Usage (wrapping the code path you’re measuring):**
```python
# Example usage inside your ingestion processing entrypoint
import time
from metrics_query_listener import collect_queries

def run_one_ingestion_and_measure(...):
    wall_start = time.perf_counter()

    with collect_queries() as qc:
        # Do the work you want to measure here:
        # - parse + validate + normalize
        # - persist panels/tests/observations
        # - anything else in the ingestion processing pipeline
        process_ingestion(...)

    wall_s = time.perf_counter() - wall_start

    print("Wall-clock end-to-end:", wall_s)
    print("Total SQL queries:", qc.query_count)
    print("Total DB time (s):", qc.total_db_time_s)
    print("Top statements by time:")
    for fp, total_s, count in qc.top_by_time(10):
        print(f"  {total_s:8.3f}s  {count:4d}x  {fp}")
```

---

**How it works (briefly)**
- `install_query_listeners(engine)` attaches two global hooks to the SQLAlchemy `Engine`.
- On every SQL execution:
  - `before_cursor_execute` saves a start timestamp on SQLAlchemy’s `context`.
  - `after_cursor_execute` computes the duration and (if a collector is active) records:
    - increment query count
    - add to total DB time
    - attribute the duration to a “fingerprint” of the SQL statement
- `collect_queries()` uses a `ContextVar` so only the specific run you wrap gets attributed metrics (everything else is ignored).

---

**Where wall-clock “end-to-end” should be measured**
- For your benchmark definition (“upload accepted → processing completed”), the most robust wall-clock measurement is **client-side** (your benchmark runner/uploader):
  - start timer before POSTing CSV
  - poll status endpoint until terminal state
  - stop timer
- The SQLAlchemy listener approach is ideal for the **server-side DB work** during processing; it complements wall-clock timing nicely.

If you want, I can implement this instrumentation in the codebase in a way that:
- turns on only in “benchmark mode” (env var),
- writes a per-ingestion summary keyed by `ingestion_id`,
- and makes it easy to scrape into your Google Sheet / CSV.

_**User (2026-03-20 19:59Z)**_

would it be too much extra work or complexity or maybe unnessary to add split by phase, such as validation, normalization?

---

_**Agent (model copilot/gpt-5.2)**_

No — splitting metrics “by phase” (validation vs normalization vs persistence) is not too much *conceptually*, but it **can** become extra work depending on how you implement it.

For a resume-focused benchmark, I’d treat it like this:

## When phase split is worth it
Do it if you expect your optimization to target a specific stage (e.g., N+1 in persistence/normalization). Phase tags let you say:

- “Total queries dropped 45%, and the drop was almost entirely in normalization writes.”

That’s a strong story.

## When it becomes unnecessary/too complex
It’s unnecessary if:
- Your main goal is just “before vs after” totals (wall-clock + query count + top fingerprints), and
- You can already see the hot queries in the “top fingerprints” list.

In that case, totals + top queries is usually enough.

## Lowest-complexity way to add phases (recommended)
Keep the SQLAlchemy listeners exactly as-is, and add a **single current phase label** that you can set around code blocks.

Conceptually:

- You keep a `ContextVar[str]` called `CURRENT_PHASE`
- Your `after_cursor_execute` records `(fingerprint, phase)` instead of just fingerprint
- You add tiny wrappers like:

```python
with phase("validation"):
    validator.validate(...)

with phase("normalization"):
    normalizer.normalize(...)
```

This adds minimal code and minimal risk, and you can still compute:
- query_count_total
- query_count_by_phase
- db_time_total
- db_time_by_phase
- top fingerprints overall and per phase

## Big warning (so you don’t get surprised)
If ingestion processing happens in a background task / different thread, you must ensure your phase context propagates correctly. Using `ContextVar` works well within one request/task, but cross-thread/process boundaries need care. If your ingestion processing is in-process background tasks (FastAPI `BackgroundTasks`), you’re likely okay; if you later move to Celery, you’d need a different propagation mechanism.

## My suggestion for your project right now
- Start with **totals + top fingerprints** only (fast, already very compelling).
- Add **phase split only if** after your first baseline you find yourself saying “I can’t tell whether validation or normalization dominates”.

If you want, I can add a short optional section to `README.md` that describes phase tagging as “nice-to-have” and keeps the core protocol simple.

PROCESSING, mark it COMPLETED (heal).\n    - Otherwise, requeue to RECEIVED and re-run processing.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n    cutoff = now - timedelta(seconds=max_age_seconds)\n\n    last_event_subq = (\n        select(\n            ProcessingEvent.ingestion_id.label(\"ingestion_id\"),\n            func.max(ProcessingEvent.occurred_at).label(\"last_occurred_at\"),\n        )\n        .group_by(ProcessingEvent.ingestion_id)\n        .subquery()\n    )\n\n    stmt = (\n        select(Ingestion.ingestion_id)\n        .outerjoin(\n            last_event_subq,\n            last_event_subq.c.ingestion_id == Ingestion.ingestion_id,\n        )\n        .where(Ingestion.status == IngestionStatus.PROCESSING)\n        .where(\n            (last_event_subq.c.last_occurred_at &lt; cutoff)\n            | (\n                (last_event_subq.c.last_occurred_at.is_(None))\n                &amp; (Ingestion.api_received_at &lt; cutoff)\n            )\n        )\n        .limit(limit)\n    )\n\n    ingestion_ids = list(session.scalars(stmt))\n\n    repo = IngestionRepository(session)\n    completed = 0\n    retried = 0\n    failed_retries = 0\n    acted_on: list[str] = []\n\n    for ingestion_id in ingestion_ids:\n        try:\n            norm_success = session.execute(\n                select(ProcessingEvent.event_id)\n                .where(ProcessingEvent.ingestion_id == ingestion_id)\n                .where(\n                    ProcessingEvent.event_type.in_(\n                        [\n                            ProcessingEventType.NORMALIZATION_SUCCEEDED,\n                            ProcessingEventType.NORMALIZATION_SUCCEEDED_WITH_WARNINGS,\n                        ]\n                    )\n                )\n                .limit(1)\n            ).first()\n\n            if norm_success is not None:\n                if not dry_run:\n                    repo.mark_completed(ingestion_id)\n                    session.commit()\n                completed += 1\n                acted_on.append(str(ingestion_id))\n                continue\n\n            if not dry_run:\n                if not repo.requeue_processing(ingestion_id):\n                    continue\n                session.commit()\n\n                svc = IngestionService(session)\n                svc.process_ingestion(ingestion_id)\n\n            retried += 1\n            acted_on.append(str(ingestion_id))\n        except Exception:\n            failed_retries += 1\n            session.rollback()\n\n    return {\n        \"considered\": len(ingestion_ids),\n        \"completed\": completed,\n        \"retried\": retried,\n        \"failed_retries\": failed_retries,\n        \"ingestion_ids\": acted_on,\n    }\n\n\ndef reap_stuck_ingestions_task(\n    *,\n    max_age_seconds: int = 15 * 60,\n    limit: int = 50,\n    dry_run: bool = False,\n) -&gt; dict[str, int | list[str]]:\n    session = Session(engine)\n    try:\n        return reap_stuck_ingestions(\n            session,\n            max_age_seconds=max_age_seconds,\n            limit=limit,\n            dry_run=dry_run,\n        )\n    finally:\n        session.close()\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
  "textEditContent": "\"\"\"Runtime metrics helpers (opt-in).\n\nThis package is intentionally lightweight and has no side effects on import.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport os\nimport re\nimport time\nfrom collections import Counter, defaultdict\nfrom contextvars import ContextVar\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import event\nfrom sqlalchemy.engine import Engine\n\n\ndef _bool_env(name: str, default: bool = False) -&gt; bool:\n    raw = os.getenv(name)\n    if raw is None:\n        return default\n    return raw.strip().lower() in {\"1\", \"true\", \"t\", \"yes\", \"y\", \"on\"}\n\n\ndef query_metrics_enabled() -&gt; bool:\n    \"\"\"Feature flag for query metrics.\n\n    Enabled via &#96;CLA_QUERY_METRICS=1&#96;.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    return _bool_env(\"CLA_QUERY_METRICS\", False)\n\n\n_WS_RE = re.compile(r\"\\s+\")\n_LITERAL_RE = re.compile(\n    r\"(?:'(?:''|[^'])*')|(?:\\b\\d+(?:\\.\\d+)?\\b)\", re.IGNORECASE\n)\n\n\ndef fingerprint_sql(statement: str) -&gt; str:\n    \"\"\"Create a coarse SQL fingerprint for grouping similar statements.\n\n    This intentionally avoids heavy dependencies. It:\n    - collapses whitespace\n    - replaces quoted strings and numeric literals with '?'\n\n    It's good enough for 'top statements' reporting.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    s = statement.strip()\n    s = _WS_RE.sub(\" \", s)\n    s = _LITERAL_RE.sub(\"?\", s)\n    return s\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass QueryCollector:\n    query_count: int = 0\n    total_db_time_s: float = 0.0\n    fingerprint_count: Counter[str] = field(default_factory=Counter)\n    fingerprint_time_s: dict[str, float] = field(\n        default_factory=lambda: defaultdict(float)\n    )\n\n    def record(self, statement: str, duration_s: float) -&gt; None:\n        fp = fingerprint_sql(statement)\n        self.query_count += 1\n        self.total_db_time_s += duration_s\n        self.fingerprint_count[fp] += 1\n        self.fingerprint_time_s[fp] += duration_s\n\n    def top_by_total_time(self, n: int = 10) -&gt; list[dict[str, Any]]:\n        items = []\n        for fp, total_time in self.fingerprint_time_s.items():\n            items.append(\n                {\n                    \"fingerprint\": fp,\n                    \"total_time_s\": total_time,\n                    \"count\": int(self.fingerprint_count.get(fp, 0)),\n                }\n            )\n        items.sort(key=lambda d: d[\"total_time_s\"], reverse=True)\n        return items[:n]\n\n    def top_by_count(self, n: int = 10) -&gt; list[dict[str, Any]]:\n        items = []\n        for fp, count in self.fingerprint_count.items():\n            items.append(\n                {\n                    \"fingerprint\": fp,\n                    \"total_time_s\": float(self.fingerprint_time_s.get(fp, 0.0)),\n                    \"count\": int(count),\n                }\n            )\n        items.sort(key=lambda d: d[\"count\"], reverse=True)\n        return items[:n]\n\n\n_ACTIVE_COLLECTOR: ContextVar[QueryCollector | None] = ContextVar(\n    \"ACTIVE_QUERY_COLLECTOR\", default=None\n)\n\n\ndef collect_queries() -&gt; \"_CollectQueries\":\n    \"\"\"Context manager that activates query collection for the current context.\"\"\"\n\n    return _CollectQueries()\n\n\nclass _CollectQueries:\n    def __init__(self) -&gt; None:\n        self.collector = QueryCollector()\n        self._token = None\n\n    def __enter__(self) -&gt; QueryCollector:\n        self._token = _ACTIVE_COLLECTOR.set(self.collector)\n        return self.collector\n\n    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb) -&gt; None:\n        if self._token is not None:\n            _ACTIVE_COLLECTOR.reset(self._token)\n\n\n_INSTALLED_ENGINES: set[int] = set()\n\n\ndef install_listeners(engine: Engine) -&gt; None:\n    \"\"\"Install SQLAlchemy listeners on the given Engine (idempotent).\"\"\"\n\n    engine_id = id(engine)\n    if engine_id in _INSTALLED_ENGINES:\n        return\n    _INSTALLED_ENGINES.add(engine_id)\n\n    @event.listens_for(engine, \"before_cursor_execute\")\n    def _before_cursor_execute(\n        conn,\n        cursor,\n        statement: str,\n        parameters: Any,\n        context,\n        executemany: bool,\n    ) -&gt; None:\n        context._cla_query_start_time_s = time.perf_counter()\n\n    @event.listens_for(engine, \"after_cursor_execute\")\n    def _after_cursor_execute(\n        conn,\n        cursor,\n        statement: str,\n        parameters: Any,\n        context,\n        executemany: bool,\n    ) -&gt; None:\n        collector = _ACTIVE_COLLECTOR.get()\n        if collector is None:\n            return\n        start = getattr(context, \"_cla_query_start_time_s\", None)\n        if start is None:\n            return\n        collector.record(statement, time.perf_counter() - start)\nfrom sqlalchemy import create_engine\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session\nfrom collections.abc import Generator\nimport os\n\n\ndef _bool_env(name: str, default: bool) -&gt; bool:\n    raw = os.getenv(name)\n    if raw is None:\n        return default\n    return raw.strip().lower() in {\"1\", \"true\", \"t\", \"yes\", \"y\", \"on\"}\n\n\ndef _default_database_url() -&gt; str:\n    if os.getenv(\"ENV\") == \"testing\":\n        return \"postgresql+psycopg://localhost:5432/test_cla\"\n    return \"postgresql+psycopg://localhost:5432/cla\"\n\n\nDATABASE_URL = os.getenv(\"DATABASE_URL\") or _default_database_url()\n\n_default_echo = os.getenv(\"ENV\") != \"testing\"\nengine = create_engine(\n    DATABASE_URL, echo=_bool_env(\"SQLALCHEMY_ECHO\", _default_echo)\n)\n\n# Optional query metrics (totals + fingerprints) for benchmark runs.\n# Enable with: CLA_QUERY_METRICS=1\nif _bool_env(\"CLA_QUERY_METRICS\", False):\n    from app.metrics.sqlalchemy_query_metrics import install_listeners\n\n    install_listeners(engine)\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport os\nimport time\nfrom uuid import UUID\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import select, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session\n\nfrom app.core.ingestion_status_enums import IngestionStatus\nfrom app.persistence.db import engine\nfrom app.persistence.models.core import Ingestion\nfrom app.persistence.models.provenance import (\n    ProcessingEvent,\n    ProcessingEventType,\n)\nfrom app.persistence.repositories.ingestion_repo import IngestionRepository\nfrom app.services.ingestion_service import IngestionService\n\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(\"uvicorn.error\")\n\n\ndef _bool_env(name: str, default: bool = False) -&gt; bool:\n    raw = os.getenv(name)\n    if raw is None:\n        return default\n    return raw.strip().lower() in {\"1\", \"true\", \"t\", \"yes\", \"y\", \"on\"}\n\n\ndef process_ingestion_task(ingestion_id: UUID) -&gt; None:\n    logger.info(\n        \"process_ingestion_task started\",\n        extra={\n            \"ingestion_id\": str(ingestion_id),\n            \"db_url\": str(engine.url),\n        },\n    )\n    session = Session(engine)\n    try:\n        ingestion = IngestionRepository(session).get_by_ingestion_id(ingestion_id)\n        if ingestion is None:\n            logger.error(\n                \"process_ingestion_task cannot see ingestion row\",\n                extra={\n                    \"ingestion_id\": str(ingestion_id),\n                    \"db_url\": str(engine.url),\n                },\n            )\n            return\n\n        svc = IngestionService(session)\n\n        if _bool_env(\"CLA_QUERY_METRICS\", False):\n            from app.metrics.sqlalchemy_query_metrics import collect_queries\n\n            wall_start = time.perf_counter()\n            with collect_queries() as qc:\n                svc.process_ingestion(ingestion_id)\n                session.commit()\n            wall_s = time.perf_counter() - wall_start\n\n            logger.info(\n                \"query_metrics\",\n                extra={\n                    \"ingestion_id\": str(ingestion_id),\n                    \"wall_time_s\": wall_s,\n                    \"sql_query_count\": qc.query_count,\n                    \"sql_total_db_time_s\": qc.total_db_time_s,\n                    \"sql_top_by_total_time\": qc.top_by_total_time(10),\n                    \"sql_top_by_count\": qc.top_by_count(10),\n                },\n            )\n        else:\n            svc.process_ingestion(ingestion_id)\n            session.commit()\n        logger.info(\n            \"process_ingestion_task finished\",\n            extra={\"ingestion_id\": str(ingestion_id)},\n        )\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.exception(\n            \"process_ingestion_task crashed\",\n            extra={\"ingestion_id\": str(ingestion_id)},\n        )\n        session.rollback()\n\n        # Persist FAILED status in a separate transaction\n        fail_session = Session(engine)\n        try:\n            IngestionRepository(fail_session).mark_failed(\n                ingestion_id=ingestion_id,\n                error_code=\"exception\",\n                error_detail={\"message\": str(e), \"type\": type(e).__name__},\n            )\n            fail_session.commit()\n        finally:\n            fail_session.close()\n    finally:\n        session.close()\n\n\ndef reap_stuck_ingestions(\n    session: Session,\n    *,\n    max_age_seconds: int = 15 * 60,\n    limit: int = 50,\n    dry_run: bool = False,\n) -&gt; dict[str, int | list[str]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Best-effort recovery for ingestions stuck in PROCESSING.\n\n    Strategy:\n    - Find PROCESSING ingestions whose latest ProcessingEvent.occurred_at is older\n      than &#96;now - max_age_seconds&#96;.\n    - If normalization already succeeded (NORMALIZATION_SUCCEEDED event present) but ingestion status is still PROCESSING, mark it COMPLETED (heal).\n    - Otherwise, requeue to RECEIVED and re-run processing.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n    cutoff = now - timedelta(seconds=max_age_seconds)\n\n    last_event_subq = (\n        select(\n            ProcessingEvent.ingestion_id.label(\"ingestion_id\"),\n            func.max(ProcessingEvent.occurred_at).label(\"last_occurred_at\"),\n        )\n        .group_by(ProcessingEvent.ingestion_id)\n        .subquery()\n    )\n\n    stmt = (\n        select(Ingestion.ingestion_id)\n        .outerjoin(\n            last_event_subq,\n            last_event_subq.c.ingestion_id == Ingestion.ingestion_id,\n        )\n        .where(Ingestion.status == IngestionStatus.PROCESSING)\n        .where(\n            (last_event_subq.c.last_occurred_at &lt; cutoff)\n            | (\n                (last_event_subq.c.last_occurred_at.is_(None))\n                &amp; (Ingestion.api_received_at &lt; cutoff)\n            )\n        )\n        .limit(limit)\n    )\n\n    ingestion_ids = list(session.scalars(stmt))\n\n    repo = IngestionRepository(session)\n    completed = 0\n    retried = 0\n    failed_retries = 0\n    acted_on: list[str] = []\n\n    for ingestion_id in ingestion_ids:\n        try:\n            norm_success = session.execute(\n                select(ProcessingEvent.event_id)\n                .where(ProcessingEvent.ingestion_id == ingestion_id)\n                .where(\n                    ProcessingEvent.event_type.in_(\n                        [\n                            ProcessingEventType.NORMALIZATION_SUCCEEDED,\n                            ProcessingEventType.NORMALIZATION_SUCCEEDED_WITH_WARNINGS,\n                        ]\n                    )\n                )\n                .limit(1)\n            ).first()\n\n            if norm_success is not None:\n                if not dry_run:\n                    repo.mark_completed(ingestion_id)\n                    session.commit()\n                completed += 1\n                acted_on.append(str(ingestion_id))\n                continue\n\n            if not dry_run:\n                if not repo.requeue_processing(ingestion_id):\n                    continue\n                session.commit()\n\n                svc = IngestionService(session)\n                svc.process_ingestion(ingestion_id)\n\n            retried += 1\n            acted_on.append(str(ingestion_id))\n        except Exception:\n            failed_retries += 1\n            session.rollback()\n\n    return {\n        \"considered\": len(ingestion_ids),\n        \"completed\": completed,\n        \"retried\": retried,\n        \"failed_retries\": failed_retries,\n        \"ingestion_ids\": acted_on,\n    }\n\n\ndef reap_stuck_ingestions_task(\n    *,\n    max_age_seconds: int = 15 * 60,\n    limit: int = 50,\n    dry_run: bool = False,\n) -&gt; dict[str, int | list[str]]:\n    session = Session(engine)\n    try:\n        return reap_stuck_ingestions(\n            session,\n            max_age_seconds=max_age_seconds,\n            limit=limit,\n            dry_run=dry_run,\n        )\n    finally:\n        session.close()\n",

from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
import pytest
from unittest.mock import patch

from app.api.routers.ingestion import router

# from datetime import datetime
import io
import uuid
import hashlib
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta


from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from app.api.routers.dependencies import get_session

# Create a client that includes the router
app = FastAPI()
app.include_router(router)


"""
POST "/ingestions" testing:

- Test the success path (202): Simulate a new, unique file upload (with and without content hash)
- Test the hash mismatch (400): Send a file where the client-provided hash doesn't match the server-calculated one.
- Test the duplicate with same content (200): Mock the database call to return an existing record with a matching hash.
- Test the duplicate with different content (409): Mock the database call to return an existing record with a different hash.
- Test the payload too large (413): Simulate a request with a Content-Length header that is too big.
"""


# Override the database dependency to use test database
@pytest.fixture
def client(db_session):
    """Create a test client with overridden database dependency."""

    def override_get_session():
        yield db_session

    app.dependency_overrides[get_session] = override_get_session
    test_client = TestClient(app)
    yield test_client
    app.dependency_overrides.clear()


# Test 202_ACCEPTED response. HTTP request variants: content hash provided and
# not provided.


# @pytest.mark.skip(reason="Works ok. Isolating other tests")
@pytest.mark.parametrize("include_content_hash", [True, False])
def test_202_success(
    client,
    valid_form_data,
    valid_csv_file,
    content_sha256,
    include_content_hash,
    db_session,
):
    # Add optional hash to the form data if this test case requires it
    if include_content_hash:
        valid_form_data["content_sha256"] = content_sha256

    response = client.post(
        "/ingestions",
        data=valid_form_data,
        files=valid_csv_file,
    )

    response_data = response.json()

    assert response.status_code == 202
    assert "ingestion_id" in response_data
    assert response_data["status"] == "RECEIVED"
    assert "Ingestion request received" in response_data["message"]

    # Checks that the ingestion_id in response is valid uuid
    try:
        uuid.UUID(response_data["ingestion_id"])
    except ValueError:
        assert False, "ingestion_id is not a valid UUID"

    # Verify database record was created
    from app.persistence.models.core import Ingestion, RawData
    from sqlalchemy import select

    ingestion_record = db_session.scalars(
        select(Ingestion).where(
            Ingestion.instrument_id == valid_form_data["instrument_id"],
            Ingestion.run_id == valid_form_data["run_id"],
        )
    ).first()

    assert ingestion_record is not None
    assert ingestion_record.instrument_id == valid_form_data["instrument_id"]
    assert ingestion_record.run_id == valid_form_data["run_id"]
    assert ingestion_record.server_sha256 == content_sha256

    # Verify raw data was stored
    raw_data_record = db_session.scalars(
        select(RawData).where(
            RawData.ingestion_id == ingestion_record.ingestion_id
        )
    ).first()

    assert raw_data_record is not None
    assert raw_data_record.content_size_bytes > 0


# Test 400 Hash Mismatch. Content hash provided by client doesn't match server
# generated hash
def test_400_hash_mismatch(client, valid_form_data, valid_csv_file):
    mismatched_hash = "incorrect_content_hash"

    valid_form_data["content_sha256"] = mismatched_hash

    response = client.post(
        "/ingestions",
        data=valid_form_data,
        files=valid_csv_file,
    )

    response_data = response.json()

    assert response.status_code == 400
    assert "CONTENT_HASH_MISMATCH" in response_data["detail"]["code"]
    assert "integrity check failed" in response_data["detail"]["message"]


# Test 200 Duplicate - OK when submit a duplicate with matching content
def test_200_duplicate_ok(
    client, valid_form_data, valid_csv_file, content_sha256, db_session
):
    # existing_ingestion_id = str(uuid.uuid4())

    # Mock the database lookup to return existing record with matching hash
    # with patch("routers.ingestion.get_existing_ingestion") as mock_db:
    #     mock_db.return_value = (existing_ingestion_id, content_sha256)

    from app.persistence.models.core import Ingestion, RawData
    from datetime import datetime
    from app.core.ingestion_status_enums import IngestionStatus

    # Create an existing ingestion and add it to the database
    existing_ingestion_id = uuid.uuid4()
    existing_record = Ingestion(
        ingestion_id=existing_ingestion_id,
        instrument_id=valid_form_data["instrument_id"],
        run_id=valid_form_data["run_id"],
        uploader_id=valid_form_data["uploader_id"],
        spec_version=valid_form_data["spec_version"],
        uploader_received_at=datetime.now(),
        api_received_at=datetime.now(),
        submitted_sha256=None,
        server_sha256=content_sha256,  # Same hash as new submission
        status=IngestionStatus.PROCESSING,
        source_filename="existing_file.csv",
    )

    db_session.add(existing_record)
    db_session.commit()
    db_session.flush()  # To make ingestion_id available for RawData

    raw_data_record = RawData(
        ingestion_id=existing_ingestion_id,
        content_bytes=b"test csv content",  # Raw file bytes
        content_size_bytes=len(b"test csv content"),
    )

    db_session.add(raw_data_record)
    db_session.commit()

    response = client.post(
        "/ingestions",
        data=valid_form_data,
        files=valid_csv_file,
    )

    response_data = response.json()

    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response_data["existing_ingestion_id"] == str(
        existing_record.ingestion_id
    )
    assert str(existing_record.ingestion_id) in response.headers["Location"]
    assert "already submitted" in response_data["message"]


# Test 409 duplicate. Duplicate error when content of the duplicate doesn't
# match existing record
# @pytest.mark.skip(reason="No database integration yet.")
def test_409_duplicate_error(
    client, valid_form_data, valid_csv_file, content_sha256, db_session
):
    # existing_ingestion_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    # existing_content_sha256 = "very_different_hash"

    # with patch("routers.ingestion.get_existing_ingestion") as mock_db:
    #     mock_db.return_value = (existing_ingestion_id, existing_content_sha256)
    from app.persistence.models.core import Ingestion, RawData
    from datetime import datetime
    from app.core.ingestion_status_enums import IngestionStatus

    # Create an existing ingestion and add it to the database. Use intentionally incorrect server_sha256
    existing_ingestion_id = uuid.uuid4()
    existing_record = Ingestion(
        ingestion_id=existing_ingestion_id,
        instrument_id=valid_form_data["instrument_id"],
        run_id=valid_form_data["run_id"],
        uploader_id=valid_form_data["uploader_id"],
        spec_version=valid_form_data["spec_version"],
        uploader_received_at=datetime.now(),
        api_received_at=datetime.now(),
        submitted_sha256=None,
        server_sha256="very_wrong_hash",  # Hash differs from the new submission
        status=IngestionStatus.PROCESSING,
        source_filename="existing_file.csv",
    )

    db_session.add(existing_record)
    db_session.commit()
    db_session.flush()  # To make ingestion_id available for RawData

    raw_data_record = RawData(
        ingestion_id=existing_ingestion_id,
        content_bytes=b"test csv content",  # Raw file bytes
        content_size_bytes=len(b"test csv content"),
    )

    db_session.add(raw_data_record)
    db_session.commit()

    response = client.post(
        "/ingestions",
        data=valid_form_data,
        files=valid_csv_file,
    )

    response_data = response.json()

    assert response.status_code == 409
    assert response_data["detail"]["code"] == "RUN_ID_CONTENT_MISMATCH"
    assert response_data["detail"]["existing_ingestion_id"] == str(
        existing_ingestion_id
    )
    assert (
        response_data["detail"]["conflict_key"]["instrument_id"]
        == valid_form_data["instrument_id"]
    )
    assert (
        response_data["detail"]["conflict_key"]["run_id"]
        == valid_form_data["run_id"]
    )
    assert (
        response_data["detail"]["hashes"]["existing"]
        == existing_record.server_sha256
    )
    assert response_data["detail"]["hashes"]["submitted"] == content_sha256
    assert (
        "ingestion already exists"
        in response_data["detail"]["message"].lower()
    )


def test_413_content_too_large(client, valid_form_data, valid_csv_file):

    response = client.post(
        "/ingestions",
        data=valid_form_data,
        files=valid_csv_file,
        headers={"Content-Length": "11000000"},  # 11 MB > 10 MB limit
    )

    response_data = response.json()

    assert response.status_code == 413
    assert response_data["detail"]["code"] == "PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE"
    assert "exceeds" in response_data["detail"]["message"].lower()
    assert response_data["detail"]["max_bytes"] == 10000000


"""
GET endpoints testing
"""


def _make_patient_id() -> str:
    return f"PAT-{uuid.uuid4()}"


def _seed_ingestion(
    db_session,
    *,
    ingestion_id: uuid.UUID | None = None,
    instrument_id: str | None = None,
    run_id: str | None = None,
    uploader_id: str = "test-uploader",
    spec_version: str = "analyzer_csv_v1",
):
    from app.persistence.models.core import Ingestion
    from app.core.ingestion_status_enums import IngestionStatus

    ingestion_id = ingestion_id or uuid.uuid4()
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    instrument_id = instrument_id or f"TEST-INSTR-{uuid.uuid4()}"
    run_id = run_id or f"TEST-RUN-{uuid.uuid4()}"

    ingestion = Ingestion(
        ingestion_id=ingestion_id,
        instrument_id=instrument_id,
        run_id=run_id,
        uploader_id=uploader_id,
        spec_version=spec_version,
        uploader_received_at=now,
        api_received_at=now,
        submitted_sha256=None,
        server_sha256="0" * 64,
        status=IngestionStatus.RECEIVED.value,
        error_code=None,
        error_detail=None,
        source_filename="seed.csv",
    )

    db_session.add(ingestion)
    db_session.commit()
    return ingestion


def test_post_process_ingestion_202_queues_task_and_returns_location(
    client, db_session
):
    ingestion = _seed_ingestion(db_session)

    with patch(
        "app.api.routers.ingestion.process_ingestion_task"
    ) as mock_task:
        response = client.post(f"/ingestions/{ingestion.ingestion_id}/process")

    assert response.status_code == 202
    assert (
        response.headers["Location"]
        == f"/v1/ingestions/{ingestion.ingestion_id}"
    )
    mock_task.assert_called_once_with(ingestion.ingestion_id)


def test_post_process_ingestion_404_when_ingestion_missing(client):
    missing_id = uuid.uuid4()

    with patch(
        "app.api.routers.ingestion.process_ingestion_task"
    ) as mock_task:
        response = client.post(f"/ingestions/{missing_id}/process")

    assert response.status_code == 404
    data = response.json()
    assert data["detail"]["code"] == "INGESTION_NOT_FOUND"
    mock_task.assert_not_called()


def test_post_process_ingestion_422_invalid_uuid(client):
    response = client.post("/ingestions/not-a-uuid/process")
    assert response.status_code == 422


def _seed_panel(
    db_session, *, ingestion_id: uuid.UUID, patient_id: str, panel_code: str
):
    from app.persistence.models.parsing import Panel

    panel = Panel(
        panel_id=uuid.uuid4(),
        ingestion_id=ingestion_id,
        patient_id=patient_id,
        panel_code=panel_code,
        sample_id=None,
        collection_timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
    )
    db_session.add(panel)
    db_session.commit()
    return panel


def _seed_test(
    db_session, *, panel_id: uuid.UUID, row_number: int, test_code: str
):
    from app.persistence.models.parsing import Test

    test = Test(
        test_id=uuid.uuid4(),
        panel_id=panel_id,
        row_number=row_number,
        test_code=test_code,
        test_name=None,
        analyte_type="NUM",
        result_raw="1",
        units_raw="mg/dL",
        result_value_num=1.0,
        result_comparator=None,
        ref_low_raw=None,
        ref_high_raw=None,
        flag=None,
    )
    db_session.add(test)
    db_session.commit()
    return test


def _seed_diagnostic_report(
    db_session,
    *,
    diagnostic_report_id: uuid.UUID,
    ingestion_id: uuid.UUID,
    panel_id: uuid.UUID,
    patient_id: str,
    panel_code: str,
    effective_at: datetime,
    resource_json: dict | None,
):
    from app.persistence.models.normalization import DiagnosticReport

    dr = DiagnosticReport(
        diagnostic_report_id=diagnostic_report_id,
        ingestion_id=ingestion_id,
        panel_id=panel_id,
        patient_id=patient_id,
        panel_code=panel_code,
        effective_at=effective_at,
        normalized_at=effective_at + timedelta(seconds=1),
        resource_json=resource_json,
        status="FINAL",
    )
    db_session.add(dr)
    db_session.commit()
    return dr


def _seed_observation(
    db_session,
    *,
    observation_id: uuid.UUID,
    test_id: uuid.UUID,
    diagnostic_report_id: uuid.UUID,
    ingestion_id: uuid.UUID,
    patient_id: str,
    code: str,
    effective_at: datetime,
    resource_json: dict | None,
):
    from app.persistence.models.normalization import Observation

    obs = Observation(
        observation_id=observation_id,
        test_id=test_id,
        diagnostic_report_id=diagnostic_report_id,
        ingestion_id=ingestion_id,
        patient_id=patient_id,
        code=code,
        display=None,
        effective_at=effective_at,
        normalized_at=effective_at + timedelta(seconds=1),
        value_num=1.0,
        value_text=None,
        comparator=None,
        unit="mg/dL",
        ref_low_num=None,
        ref_high_num=None,
        flag_analyzer_interpretation=None,
        flag_system_interpretation=None,
        discrepancy=None,
        status="FINAL",
        resource_json=resource_json,
    )
    db_session.add(obs)
    db_session.commit()
    return obs


def _seed_patient_resources(
    db_session,
    *,
    patient_id: str,
    ingestion_id: uuid.UUID | None = None,
    instrument_id: str | None = None,
    run_id: str | None = None,
    uploader_id: str = "test-uploader",
    spec_version: str = "analyzer_csv_v1",
):
    """Seeds 4 DR + 4 Obs for a patient (and the required Panel/Test rows).

    Returns a dict with keys:
    ingestion_id, panels, diagnostic_reports, observations
    """

    ingestion = _seed_ingestion(
        db_session,
        ingestion_id=ingestion_id,
        instrument_id=instrument_id,
        run_id=run_id,
        uploader_id=uploader_id,
        spec_version=spec_version,
    )
    base_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    panels = []
    diagnostic_reports = []
    observations = []
    for i in range(4):
        panel_code = f"PANEL-{i}"
        panel = _seed_panel(
            db_session,
            ingestion_id=ingestion.ingestion_id,
            patient_id=patient_id,
            panel_code=panel_code,
        )
        panels.append(panel)

        # Newer effective_at first (patient endpoints sort DESC).
        effective_at = base_time - timedelta(minutes=i)

        dr_id = uuid.UUID(int=i + 1)
        dr_resource = {"resourceType": "DiagnosticReport", "id": str(dr_id)}
        dr = _seed_diagnostic_report(
            db_session,
            diagnostic_report_id=dr_id,
            ingestion_id=ingestion.ingestion_id,
            panel_id=panel.panel_id,
            patient_id=patient_id,
            panel_code=panel_code,
            effective_at=effective_at,
            resource_json=dr_resource,
        )
        diagnostic_reports.append(dr)

        test = _seed_test(
            db_session,
            panel_id=panel.panel_id,
            row_number=i + 1,
            test_code=f"TEST-{i}",
        )

        obs_id = uuid.UUID(int=100 + i)
        obs_resource = {"resourceType": "Observation", "id": str(obs_id)}
        obs = _seed_observation(
            db_session,
            observation_id=obs_id,
            test_id=test.test_id,
            diagnostic_report_id=dr.diagnostic_report_id,
            ingestion_id=ingestion.ingestion_id,
            patient_id=patient_id,
            code=f"CODE-{i}",
            effective_at=effective_at,
            resource_json=obs_resource,
        )
        observations.append(obs)

    return {
        "ingestion_id": ingestion.ingestion_id,
        "panels": panels,
        "diagnostic_reports": diagnostic_reports,
        "observations": observations,
    }


def test_get_ingestion_200(client, db_session):
    ingestion = _seed_ingestion(db_session)

    response = client.get(f"/ingestions/{ingestion.ingestion_id}")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    data = response.json()
    assert data["ingestion_id"] == str(ingestion.ingestion_id)
    assert data["status"] == "RECEIVED"
    assert "api_received_at" in data


def test_get_ingestion_404(client):
    missing_id = uuid.uuid4()
    response = client.get(f"/ingestions/{missing_id}")
    assert response.status_code == 404
    data = response.json()
    assert data["detail"]["ingestion_id"] == str(missing_id)


def test_get_ingestion_422_invalid_uuid(client):
    response = client.get("/ingestions/not-a-uuid")
    assert response.status_code == 422


def test_get_diagnostic_reports_by_ingestion_empty_list_when_ingestion_exists(
    client, db_session
):
    ingestion = _seed_ingestion(db_session)
    response = client.get(
        f"/ingestions/{ingestion.ingestion_id}/diagnostic-reports"
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == []


@pytest.mark.parametrize("include_json", [0, 1])
def test_get_diagnostic_reports_by_ingestion_include_json(
    client, db_session, include_json
):
    patient_id = _make_patient_id()
    seeded = _seed_patient_resources(db_session, patient_id=patient_id)
    ingestion_id = seeded["ingestion_id"]

    response = client.get(
        f"/ingestions/{ingestion_id}/diagnostic-reports?include_json={include_json}"
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    rows = response.json()
    assert len(rows) == 4
    # Ingestion endpoint orders by diagnostic_report_id asc.
    assert rows[0]["diagnostic_report_id"] == str(uuid.UUID(int=1))
    if include_json == 1:
        assert rows[0]["resource_json"]["resourceType"] == "DiagnosticReport"
    else:
        assert "resource_json" not in rows[0]


def test_get_diagnostic_reports_by_ingestion_include_json_invalid_422(
    client, db_session
):
    ingestion = _seed_ingestion(db_session)
    response = client.get(

    )
    assert response.status_code == 422


def test_get_diagnostic_reports_by_ingestion_404_when_ingestion_missing(
    client,
):

    response = client.get(f"/ingestions/{missing_id}/diagnostic-reports")
    assert response.status_code == 404
    data = response.json()
    assert data["detail"]["ingestion_id"] == str(missing_id)


def test_get_observations_by_ingestion_empty_list_when_ingestion_exists(
    client, db_session
):
    ingestion = _seed_ingestion(db_session)
    response = client.get(f"/ingestions/{ingestion.ingestion_id}/observations")

    assert response.json() == []


@pytest.mark.parametrize("include_json", [0, 1])
def test_get_observations_by_ingestion_pagination_and_include_json(
    client, db_session, include_json
):
    patient_id = _make_patient_id()
    seeded = _seed_patient_resources(db_session, patient_id=patient_id)
    ingestion_id = seeded["ingestion_id"]

    # Default pagination returns all 4.
    response = client.get(
        f"/ingestions/{ingestion_id}/observations?include_json={include_json}"
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    rows = response.json()
    assert len(rows) == 4
    # Ingestion endpoint orders by observation_id asc.
    assert rows[0]["observation_id"] == str(uuid.UUID(int=100))
    if include_json == 1:
        assert rows[0]["resource_json"]["resourceType"] == "Observation"
    else:
        assert "resource_json" not in rows[0]

    # limit and offset query parameters provided
    response2 = client.get(
        f"/ingestions/{ingestion_id}/observations?include_json={include_json}&limit=1&offset=1"
    )
    assert response2.status_code == 200
    rows2 = response2.json()
    assert len(rows2) == 1
    assert rows2[0]["observation_id"] == str(uuid.UUID(int=101))

    # Offset beyond total returns empty list (still code 200)
    response3 = client.get(
        f"/ingestions/{ingestion_id}/observations?include_json={include_json}&limit=10&offset=999"
    )
    assert response3.status_code == 200
    assert response3.json() == []

)

@pytest.mark.parametrize("include_json", [0, 1])
def test_get_diagnostic_reports_by_patient_pagination_and_include_json(
    client, db_session, include_json
):
    patient_id = _make_patient_id()
    seeded = _seed_patient_resources(db_session, patient_id=patient_id)

    response = client.get(
        f"/patients/{patient_id}/diagnostic-reports?include_json={include_json}"
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    rows = response.json()
    assert len(rows) == 4

    # Patient endpoint orders by effective_at desc (newest first).
    # Our seeder makes i=0 newest.
    assert rows[0]["panel_code"] == "PANEL-0"
    if include_json == 1:
        assert rows[0]["resource_json"]["resourceType"] == "DiagnosticReport"
    else:
        assert "resource_json" not in rows[0]

    # Limit and offset query parameters provided
    response2 = client.get(
        f"/patients/{patient_id}/diagnostic-reports?include_json={include_json}&limit=1&offset=1"
    )
    assert response2.status_code == 200
    rows2 = response2.json()
    assert len(rows2) == 1
    assert rows2[0]["panel_code"] == "PANEL-1"

I’ll add a small, opt-in query-metrics module (totals + fingerprints) and wire it to the SQLAlchemy `Engine` plus the ingestion processing path behind an env var, so you can turn it on for benchmark runs without affecting normal behavior.**Planning file structure and testing**

I need to provide a top 10 list in a certain format, ensuring it meets the JSON expectations of the logger. Running mypy might not be necessary, but I plan to run unit tests quickly. The user mentioned that a file changed since the last request, particularly regarding the csv_generator, so I need to implement a new file. I’ll check for the `app/metrics` directory, since I see a metrics folder outside app but not within it. I'll create `app/metrics/__init__.py` and add the necessary files. Let's move forward!Now I’m going to implement an opt-in SQLAlchemy query-metrics collector (totals + fingerprints) in `app/metrics/`, wire it to the shared `engine`, and wrap ingestion processing so each run can emit a compact summary when the feature flag is enabled.Implementation is in place; next I’ll run a quick `pytest -q` plus a fast import check to ensure the new module and env-gated imports don’t break anything when metrics are disabled (default).**Debugging test inconsistencies**

I'm trying to figure out why the tests are failing despite no changes on our part. One test expects a 413 response for a 3MB upload, but it's returning a 202. It appears the MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES may have changed to 10MB, while the test still references a 1MB limit. This mismatch possibly suggests the test is outdated, even though it passed before. We need to ensure our changes align with expected outcomes to keep everything functioning smoothly.**Resolving test failures**

I’m noticing that our running tests are failing, which may indicate the MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES limit was previously set to 1MB and has since changed—though not due to our patch. We need to ensure our changes don't break the tests. To resolve this, I have two options: I can either adjust the constant to 1MB or update the test to reflect the 10MB limit. Given that the test seems to indicate a 1MB expectation, I’ll adjust MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES to 1,000,000 to align with the tests. Fixing this is essential, even if it seems unrelated to the feature we’re implementing.**Adjusting for file size limits**

I'm considering how our changes could potentially break end-to-end tests or metrics due to file sizes larger than 1MB. The benchmarking with a CSV that has 10,800 rows might exceed this limit, particularly as the metrics protocol uses large batches that could easily surpass 1MB. Since the tests currently expect a 1MB limit, it feels reasonable to adjust them to reflect a more practical 10MB limit. Let's go ahead and inspect the tests in test_ingestion.py to ensure everything aligns properly, especially for the test_413 case.One unit test is failing because it assumes a 1MB max upload size, but the API currently enforces a 10MB limit (`MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES = 10_000_000`). I’ll update the test to exceed 10MB so it matches the real limit (and keeps your large benchmark CSVs workable).**Analyzing test results**

I'm thinking it might be because the test is running on a different engine. However, most of the tests are passing, which is good. There’s just this one test, number 413, that isn’t passing. So, I'll need to apply a patch to address the issue. It’s always interesting to figure these things out and make sure everything runs smoothly! Let's see how this patch helps.Now I’ll rerun the test suite to ensure everything is green with metrics disabled (default), and then I’ll show you how to enable the query metrics for a benchmark run via `CLA_QUERY_METRICS=1`.**Considering benchmarks and tasks**

I’m thinking about the next steps for benchmarks with the metrics environment variable. Should I propose adding a small doc note and a script to parse logs? I can't move forward without a request. I also want to check if there are any changed files beyond the CSVs since get_changed_files might be limited. I’ll note that instrumentation is enabled with the env variable, so should I ask if the user wants me to update the task to include this?I can see your working tree currently has newly generated benchmark CSVs (e.g. `small.csv`, `warmup.csv`, plus files under ``).

Before I touch anything, quick check so I don’t make the wrong move:
- Do you want these CSVs committed to the repo as fixed datasets, or should they be treated as generated artifacts (and added to `.gitignore` / removed from git)?
- Next step wise: do you want me to wire `CLA_QUERY_METRICS=1` into the existing VS Code task (or add a second “API: fastapi dev (metrics)” task), so your benchmark runs automatically emit the query totals/fingerprints?
- Are you ready to run a first warmup + 1 measured “small” run now, or do you want a tiny helper to extract the logged metrics into a single summary line per run?

str, default: int) -&gt; int:\n    raw = os.getenv(name)\n    if raw is None:\n        return default\n    try:\n        return int(raw.strip())\n    except ValueError:\n        return default\n\n\ndef _sanitize_cell(value: Any, *, max_chars: int) -&gt; str:\n    if value is None:\n        return \"\"\n    s = str(value)\n    s = s.replace(\"\\r\\n\", \" \").replace(\"\\n\", \" \").replace(\"\\r\", \" \")\n    if len(s) &gt; max_chars:\n        return s[: max_chars - 3] + \"...\"\n    return s\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass BenchmarkTopItem:\n    fingerprint: str\n it in raw_items:\n        if not isinstance(it, dict):\n            continue\n        fp = it.get(\"fingerprint\")\n        if fp is None:\n            continue\n        items.append(\n            BenchmarkTopItem(\n                fingerprint=str(fp),\n                total_time_s=float(it.get(\"total_time_s\") or 0.0),\n                count=int(it.get(\"count\") or 0),\n            )\n        )\n    return items\n\n\ndef benchmark_results_csv_path() -&gt; str | None:\n    \"\"\"Where to append benchmark rows.\n\n    When set, each processed ingestion appends a single CSV row suitable \"api_received_at_utc\",\n        \"end_to_end_s\",\n        \"wall_time_s\",\n        \"sql_query_count\",\n        \"sql_total_db_time_s\",\n    ]\n\n    cols: list[str] = []\n    for i in range(1, top_n + 1):\n        cols.extend(\n            [\n                f\"sql_top_time_{i}_fingerprint\",\n                f\"sql_top_time_{i}_total_time_s\",\n                f\"sql_top_time_{i}_count\",\n            ]\n        )\n    for i in range(1, top_n + 1):\n        cols.extend(\n            [\n                f\"sql_top_count_{i}_fingerprint\",\n                f\"sql_top_count_{i}_total_time_s\",\n                f\"sql_top_count_{i}_count\",\n            ]\n        )\n\n    return base + cols\n\n\ndef append_benchmark_row(\n    *,\n    csv_path: str,\n    measured_at: datetime,\n    git_sha: str | None,\n    api_base_url: str | None,\n    dataset: str | None,\n    source_filename: str | None,\n    ingestion_id: str,\n    instrument_id: str | None,\n content_size_bytes: int | None,\n    server_sha256: str | None,\n    submitted_sha256: str | None,\n    uploader_received_at: datetime | None,\n    api_received_at: datetime | None,\n    end_to_end_s: float | None,\n    wall_time_s: float | None,\n    sql_query_count: int | None,\n    sql_total_db_time_s: float | None,\n    sql_top_by_total_time: Any = None,\n    sql_top_by_count: Any = None,\n) -&gt; None:\n    \"\"\"Append a single benchmark row to a results CSV.\n\n    Designed to be:\n    - stable column order (good for Sheets import)\n    - safe for concurrent append within one host (uses file lock when available)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    top_n = _int_env(\"CLA_BENCHMARK_TOP_N\", _DEFAULT_TOP_N)\n    fp_max = _int_env(\"CLA_BENCHMARK_FP_MAX_CHARS\", _DEFAULT_FP_MAX_CHARS)\n\n    top_time = _coerce_top_items(sql_top_by_total_time)\n    top_count = _coerce_top_items(sql_top_by_count)\n\n    row: dict[str, Any] = {\n        \"measured_at_utc\": measured_at.isoformat(),\n        \"git_sha\": git_sha or \"\",\n        \"api_base_url\": api_base_url or \"\",\n        \"dataset\": dataset or \"\",\n        \"source_filename\": source_filename or \"\",\n        \"ingestion_id\": ingestion_id,\n        \"instrument_id\": instrument_id or \"\",\n        \"run_id\": run_id or \"\",\n        \"uploader_id\": uploader_id or \"\",\n        \"spec_version\": spec_version or \"\",\n content_size_bytes is None else int(content_size_bytes),\n        \"server_sha256\": server_sha256 or \"\",\n        \"submitted_sha256\": submitted_sha256 or \"\",\n        \"uploader_received_at_utc\": \"\" if uploader_received_at is None else uploader_received_at.isoformat(),\n        \"api_received_at_utc\": \"\" if api_received_at is None else api_received_at.isoformat(),\n        \"end_to_end_s\": \"\" if end_to_end_s is None else float(end_to_end_s),\n        \"wall_time_s\": \"\" if wall_time_s is None else float(wall_time_s),\n        \"sql_query_count\": \"\" if sql_query_count is None else int(sql_query_count),\n        \"sql_total_db_time_s\": \"\" if sql_total_db_time_s is None else float(sql_total_db_time_s),\n    }\n\n    for i in range(1, top_n + 1):\n        if i &lt;= len(top_time):\n            it = top_time[i - 1]\n            row[f\"sql_top_time_{i}_fingerprint\"] = _sanitize_cell(\n                it.fingerprint, max_chars=fp_max\n            )\n            row[f\"sql_top_time_{i}_total_time_s\"] = float(it.total_time_s)\n            row[f\"sql_top_time_{i}_count\"] = int(it.count)\n        else:\n            row[f\"sql_top_time_{i}_fingerprint\"] = \"\"\n            row[f\"sql_top_time_{i}_total_time_s\"] = \"\"\n            row[f\"sql_top_time_{i}_count\"] = \"\"\n\n    for i in range(1, top_n + 1):\n        if i &lt;= len(top_count):\n            it = top_count[i - 1]\n            row[f\"sql_top_count_{i}_fingerprint\"] = _sanitize_cell(\n                it.fingerprint, max_chars=fp_max\n            )\n            row[f\"sql_top_count_{i}_total_time_s\"] = float(it.total_time_s)\n            row[f\"sql_top_count_{i}_count\"] = int(it.count)\n        else:\n            row[f\"sql_top_count_{i}_fingerprint\"] = \"\"\n            row[f\"sql_top_count_{i}_total_time_s\"] = \"\"\n            row[f\"sql_top_count_{i}_count\"] = \"\"\n\n    path = Path(csv_path)\n    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    fieldnames = benchmark_fieldnames(top_n=top_n)\n\n    # Use a lock for safe concurrent append on Unix.\n    # On platforms without fcntl (or if it fails), we still attempt a best-effort append.\n    with path.open(\"a+\", newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        if fcntl is not None:  # pragma: no cover (covered on Unix)\n            try:\n                fcntl.flock(f.fileno(), fcntl.LOCK_EX)\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n\n        f.seek(0, os.SEEK_END)\n        need_header = f.tell() == 0\n\n        writer = csv.DictWriter(\n            f,\n            fieldnames=fieldnames,\n            extrasaction=\"ignore\",\n        )\n        if need_header:\n            writer.writeheader()\n        writer.writerow(row)\n        f.flush()\n\n        if fcntl is not None:  # pragma: no cover\n            try:\n                fcntl.flock(f.fileno(), fcntl.LOCK_UN)\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n",
